title: Thai-Style Boiled Curry Tenderloin with Fresh Tomatoes
description: A juicy and flavorful Thai-inspired dish made with tenderloin, fresh tomatoes, and a rich curry sauce.
instructions: 2. Season the tenderloin with salt and black pepper.
3. In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
4. Sear the tenderloin until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
5. Remove the tenderloin from the pot and set it aside.
6. Add the chopped tomatoes and curry to the pot, stirring to combine.
7. Pour in the boiled water and bring the mixture to a simmer.
8. Return the tenderloin to the pot and cover with a lid.
9. Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the tenderloin for 15-20 minutes, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
10. Remove the pot from the oven and let the tenderloin rest for 5 minutes.
11. Slice the tenderloin and serve with the curry sauce spooned over the top. Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
